Tampa Stand Your Ground Statute Lawyer
# Tampa Stand Your Ground Statute Lawyer
## Stand Your Ground Statute Attorney in Tampa, FL
### Criminal Defense Lawyer Representing Residents of Tampa
The Stand Your Ground law was enacted by the Florida Legislature in 2005 and received nationwide attention in connection with the death of Trayvon Martin. If you felt that you needed to defend yourself by means of deadly force, and you were charged with a crime like murder or aggravated battery with a deadly weapon, you may have a reason to raise the Stand Your Ground statute as a defense. At Hanlon Law, we provide aggressive, experienced legal counsel and representation. Tampa [criminal defense](https://www.criminalattorneytampa.net/violent-crimes.html) lawyer Will Hanlon can look at the facts and circumstances surrounding the charges and determine whether this would be a strong defense.
### The Stand Your Ground Statute in Florida
Florida Statutes section 776.012 is also known as the Stand Your Ground law. It provides that someone is justified in using deadly force and owes no duty to retreat when either:
  * The defendant reasonably believed that force was necessary to stop imminent death or great bodily harm or to stop a forcible felony from being committed imminently; or
  * The defendant acted under the circumstances described in section 776.013 in connection with a home or vehicle invasion.


Section 776.013 provides that someone in a dwelling or residence in which they have the right to be present has no duty of retreat and can stand their ground and use non-deadly force against someone else if they reasonably believe that it is necessary to prevent being killed or suffering great bodily harm, or to protect someone else from being killed or suffering great bodily harm, or to stop a forcible felony from being imminently committed.
Moreover, there is a presumption that someone had a reasonable fear of imminently being killed or seriously injured when using or threatening to use defensive force that is likely or intended to seriously injure or kill someone else if the person against whom the defendant used force was in the process of illegally and forcefully going into either a home or an occupied vehicle. It is also presumed when the alleged victim removed or was trying to remove someone else non-consensually from the home or occupied vehicle. Finally, it is presumed if you used or threatened to use defensive force when you knew or had reason to believe that the other person was unlawfully and forcibly entering or that such an act had occurred.
The presumption does not apply, however, if the alleged victim had the right to be inside the home or vehicle, and there was not an injunction for protection from domestic violence or a no-contact order in place against that person. Suppose that you and the father of your child had a violent relationship, owned a home jointly, and separated, but no injunction or no-contact order was sought. You just parted ways. The father came back to pick up his stuff, but you had changed the lock, so he forced open a window. You might not be able to use the Stand Your Ground defense successfully because he had a right to be there as an owner, and there was no no-contact order to restrict him.
Similarly, the presumption would not apply if the person or people who are being removed against their will are children or grandchildren who are in the lawful custody or under the lawful guardianship of the person against whom you used force. Using the same example as above, if the father was the legal guardian of the children, and you had refused to return the children after visitation, so he forcibly entered to take them back, you probably could not successfully raise the Stand Your Ground defense.
Other exceptions involve engaging in criminal activity while using "defensive force" or using defensive force against a law enforcement officer who is trying to get into a home or car in performance of official duties if the officer has identified himself or herself or you knew or reasonably should have known that they were a law enforcement officer.
### Consult a Skillful Gun Crime Attorney in the Tampa Area
The Stand Your Ground law continues to be controversial in Tampa and elsewhere, but it can be a very strong defense when it is appropriately applied. It is crucial to the outcome of your case to have an experienced [gun crime](https://www.criminalattorneytampa.net/gun-crimes.html) attorney advocating on your behalf and raising appropriate defenses as early as possible. Our founder, Will Hanlon, has provided a strong, aggressive defense to people accused of crimes such as aggravated battery, domestic violence, and manslaughter since 1994. He strives to provide responsive and personalized representation. Call Hanlon Law at 813-228-7095 or use our [online form](https://www.criminalattorneytampa.net/contact-us.html) to set up an appointment with a Tampa lawyer.
## Resisting an Officer Without Violence Attorney in Tampa, FL
### Attorneys Advocating for Criminal Defendants in Tampa
In Florida, it is a crime to resist a law enforcement officer with or without violence. Although the penalties are more severe for resisting with violence, you should take a charge of nonviolently resisting an officer seriously. Sometimes people resist an officer because they believe that leaving the scene or running away will cause a problem to disappear. Sometimes resisting an officer without violence is a charge brought by a prosecutor to supplement other criminal charges. At Hanlon Law, Tampa [criminal defense](https://www.criminalattorneytampa.net/violent-crimes.html) lawyer Will Hanlon provides knowledgeable legal representation and can look at the total facts and circumstances to determine which defenses would be appropriate.
### Resisting an Officer Without Violence
Resisting an officer nonviolently can include warning others that the police are coming, lying, providing a false identity or identification, fleeing after being told to stop, not getting up when asked, or not placing your hands behind your back when asked. It can include any refusal to obey verbal commands. Often, actions that form the basis for this charge can seem minor to the defendant. However, more serious actions of resistance can include concealing evidence, evading the police when they have a reasonable suspicion of criminal wrongdoing, inciting others to interfere with police activities, or presenting an invalid identification upon a lawful arrest.
In order to prove the crime of resisting an officer, the prosecutor must prove beyond a reasonable doubt that you opposed, obstructed, or resisted a law enforcement officer who was involved in executing legal process or a legal duty at the time, and you knew that the person whom you were opposing, obstructing, or resisting was an officer or another person legally authorized to execute legal process.
Officers under this code section can include police officers, county probation officers, personnel from the Department of Law Enforcement, or members of the Florida Commission on Offender Review.
If the prosecutor establishes those four elements, and you did not do violence to the officer or threaten to do violence to the officer, you can be convicted of a misdemeanor in the first degree. As a first-degree misdemeanor, you can be sentenced to a maximum of one year in jail and $1,000 in fines. In most cases, it will be less, but it is important to realize that if you do not present a successful defense, you will have a permanent criminal record and probably a term of probation. When there are aggravating circumstances, it is likely that the prosecutor will seek jail time. Moreover, if you have a criminal record already or a history of resisting arrest, it is very likely that you will go to jail if you are convicted.
An experienced attorney can make a difference to the outcome of your case. In some cases, your lawyer may be able to negotiate to have the charge dropped or to get some sort of plea deal. In other cases, an attorney can present a successful defense. Some common defenses include arguing that you did not actually oppose or obstruct a law enforcement officer, you had an involuntary physical response that caused you to tense up during the arrest or jerk away, an officer was not acting under a legal duty or not acting in a lawful way, an officer failed to explain the arrest, or an officer conducted an unlawful arrest or detention.
Generally, words alone are not enough to be considered resistance under the First Amendment, but there are situations in which your words can result in liability under this code section, such as when an officer asks for help, legally detains you, or tries to serve process. Similarly, the First Amendment will not protect you if an officer sees a crime and tries to make an arrest, but you warn the suspect about the police in order to stop the suspect from being apprehended. Other affirmative falsehoods during arrest are also not protected by the First Amendment because they hinder the officer's ability to do their job.
### Retain a Criminal Attorney in the Tampa Area to Protect Your Rights
It is unlawful to resist an officer without violence. The prosecutor will learn the officer's version of what happened when deciding whether to pursue charges, and it can be important to your defense for the prosecutor to also hear your version of what happened early in the process of deciding whether to pursue charges. Our founder, Will Hanlon, is committed to protecting the rights of the accused in the Tampa area and has represented criminal defendants since 1994. Tampa Statutory Rape Lawyer
# Tampa Statutory Rape Lawyer
## Statutory Rape Attorney in Tampa, FL
###  Tampa Attorney Advocating for Defendants Facing Sex Crime Charges 
Under Florida Statutes § 794.05, unlawful sexual activity with certain minors is punishable by law. Experienced [sex crime](https://www.criminalattorneytampa.net/sex-crimes.html) lawyer Will Hanlon at Hanlon Law has been representing people accused of statutory rape and other sex crimes throughout the Tampa Bay area for more than 20 years. He understands not only how legally complex these cases may be but also how devastating a conviction is for the accused and their family. As a Tampa statutory rape defense lawyer, he works hard in pursuit of a fair outcome for each client under the circumstances of their specific case.
Florida’s statutory scheme concerning sex crimes consists of three major branches: lewd or lascivious acts, sexual battery, and unlawful sex with minors. Whereas the crime traditionally known as “rape” is included within the broader category of sexual battery, the offense often known as “statutory rape” is a crime that falls under the category of unlawful sex with minors. “Rape” is defined primarily by whether or not the alleged victim consented to the sexual act in question. “Statutory rape” is a crime simply because the statute says it is a crime. In most cases, the same sexual act between the same two people would be legal if one of them were not a minor.
###  Defining the Crime of Statutory Rape Under Florida Law 
Lack of consent is not an element of the crime of statutory rape. Section 794.05 outlaws even consensual sexual activity (oral, anal, or vaginal penetration or union) between people who are 16 or 17 years of age and people who are age 24 or older. There are some possible defenses to the crime of unlawful sexual activity with certain minors, and a careful exploration of your situation with a statutory rape defense attorney in Tampa may illuminate whether any of them applies to you. However, the statute makes clear that neither the victim’s consent nor their prior sexual conduct is among these defenses. Likewise, the defendant’s belief regarding the victim’s age is not relevant to the crime of statutory rape. Even if the defendant honestly thought that the person with whom they engaged in a sexual act was at least 18, this is not a defense. This is true even if the alleged “victim” lied about their age.
Unlawful sexual activity with a minor is a serious criminal offense that may result in a conviction of a second-degree felony, a prison sentence of up to 15 years, and up to $10,000 in fines. The defendant may also be placed on sex offender probation and be required to register as a sex offender. In situations in which there were four years or less between the defendant’s and the victim’s ages, it may be possible to avoid registration as a sex offender under the so-called “Romeo and Juliet” law, but the defendant will still be convicted of statutory rape and sentenced accordingly.
Given the harshness of Florida’s sex with a minor laws, it is extremely important that a person accused of statutory rape talk to a knowledgeable attorney about their situation as soon as possible after being arrested or learning that they may be under criminal investigation. False accusations are not uncommon in these types of cases and may be motivated by an alleged victim’s jealousy or mental illness. Child custody disputes may also lead one parent to accuse the other parent – or the other parent’s spouse or partner – of a sex crime.
###  Hire a Knowledgeable Statutory Rape Defense Lawyer in Tampa 
While it is not always possible to avoid a conviction in a statutory rape case, the chances of having the charges reduced or dismissed are generally much better if the defendant receives quality legal representation. A public defender is not likely to spend the considerable amount of care, time, and attention that such a case requires in order to fully protect the defendant’s rights. Tampa statutory rape defense attorney Will Hanlon does everything legally possible to help his clients fight unfounded charges and protect their constitutional rights during an arrest, investigation, or trial for a sex crime or another offense. Call us at 813-228-7095 or [contact us](https://www.criminalattorneytampa.net/contact-us.html) online to set up an appointment with a [rape defense](https://www.criminalattorneytampa.net/sexual-battery.html) lawyer.

Tampa Lawyer for Exposing Minors to Harmful Media Materials
# Tampa Lawyer for Exposing Minors to Harmful Media Materials
## Legal Consequences of Exposing Minors to Harmful Media Materials in Tampa, FL
The tragic reality in our society is that children have more access than ever to a wide array of media content. While this provides educational opportunities and entertainment, it also presents a significant risk: the exposure of minors to harmful media materials. Such exposure, whether intentional or due to negligence, can have legal consequences for individuals, guardians, educators, and businesses. Across jurisdictions, laws are evolving to address these risks, emphasizing the duty of care owed to minors and the serious repercussions of violating it. The need for a Tampa Lawyer for exposing minors to harmful media materials is needed now more than ever before. 
## Defining Harmful Media
[_Harmful media materials_](https://www.flhouse.gov/Sections/bills/billsdetail.aspx?billid=82296) typically refer to content that is sexually explicit, violent, or otherwise inappropriate for children. This may include pornography, graphic violence, hate speech, or materials that promote self-harm, substance abuse, or criminal behavior. The legal definitions can vary by country or state, but most laws rely on community standards and expert input to determine what constitutes harm, especially in the context of a minor’s psychological development. 
## Federal and State-Level Protections
In the United States, there are multiple federal laws aimed at protecting minors from harmful media. One of the most significant is the [_Children’s Internet Protection Act (CIPA)_](https://www.fcc.gov/consumers/guides/childrens-internet-protection-act), which mandates that schools and libraries receiving federal funding must implement internet safety policies and filter harmful online content. Additionally, the Child Online Protection Act (COPA) and [_Children’s Online Privacy Protection Act (COPPA)_](https://www.ftc.gov/legal-library/browse/rules/childrens-online-privacy-protection-rule-coppa) provide guidelines on what kind of content can be shown to children and how their data can be collected.
On the state level, laws often impose criminal penalties on individuals who knowingly expose minors to obscene materials. For example, many states have statutes criminalizing the distribution of pornography to minors, regardless of whether it is done in person, via mail, or through digital means. Depending on the severity and the intent, such offenses can result in fines, probation, mandatory sex offender registration, or imprisonment.
## Legal Liability and Civil Consequences
Beyond criminal charges, individuals and entities can face civil lawsuits for exposing minors to harmful media. For instance, a teacher or school administrator who fails to supervise online activities might be found liable if a student accesses damaging content. Similarly, tech companies and streaming platforms can be sued for failing to implement adequate parental controls or for allowing children access to age-inappropriate content.
Parents and guardians might also bear some legal responsibility under the doctrine of negligent supervision. If a minor is harmed as a result of exposure to harmful media, such as imitating dangerous stunts or developing psychological trauma, the courts may assess whether the guardian acted reasonably in monitoring media consumption. 
## Emerging Legal Challenges
As social media platforms and AI-generated content become more prevalent, legal systems are grappling with new forms of exposure. Deepfakes, virtual reality experiences, and algorithmically driven recommendations all pose novel risks. Lawmakers are beginning to draft regulations requiring digital platforms to conduct regular content audits, enforce age verification, and provide more transparent reporting to parents and regulators.
For example, states like California have introduced legislation mandating “age-appropriate design codes” to protect children online.
## Consult With Attorney Will Hanlon Today
Exposing minors to harmful media materials is not only ethically troubling but it also carries serious legal consequences. Whether it’s through criminal prosecution, civil litigation, or regulatory enforcement, the legal system is clear in its stance: the safety and well-being of minors must be safeguarded. As technology continues to evolve, so too must the laws and practices designed to shield children from harm. Individuals, families, educators, and corporations all share a collective responsibility to ensure that minors engage with media in a safe and appropriate manner.
If you or someone you know has been accused of exposing minors to harmful media materials, seek the professional legal assistance of [_Attorney Will Hanlon_](https://www.criminalattorneytampa.net/william-hanlon.html) for the best possible defense. The right representation can make all the difference when it comes to securing a fair resolution.

Tampa Child Pornography Investigations Lawyer
# Tampa Child Pornography Investigations Lawyer
## Child Pornography Investigations in Tampa, FL
Child pornography offenses are among the most heinous crimes, and law enforcement agencies in Tampa, Florida, are committed to investigating and prosecuting such cases rigorously. These investigations involve a series of methodical steps to identify offenders, gather evidence, and ensure that justice is served. If you are facing charges after being investigated for creation or distribution of child pornography in Tampa, Florida, it is important that you understand the repercussions of this criminal offense and reach out to a Tampa child pornography investigations lawyer.
## Initiation of Investigations
Investigations often commence based on tips from various sources, including the [_National Center for Missing and Exploited Children (NCMEC)_](https://www.missingkids.org/home) CyberTipline, reports from internet service providers, or proactive monitoring by law enforcement. The [_Hillsborough County Sheriff's Office_](https://www.teamhcso.com/) detectives can use a cyber tip from NCMEC regarding suspicious activity at residences where child pornography is being distributed from. Upon consenting to a search, investigators may discover hundreds of child pornography images and videos on electronic devices belonging to the investigated resident. That would subsequently lead to an arrest on potential multiple charges related to child pornography.
## Execution of Search Warrants
Once sufficient preliminary evidence is gathered, law enforcement agencies obtain search warrants to seize electronic devices suspected of containing illicit material. You can expect agents from the [_Florida Department of Law Enforcement (FDLE)_](https://oppaga.fl.gov/ProgramSummary/ProgramDetail?programNumber=1075) to execute a search warrant at the Tampa residence of the suspects. If the search uncovers child sexual abuse material on any or all electronic devices, this would lead to an arrest.
## Collaboration Among Several Local Agencies
Investigations often involve collaboration among local, state, and federal agencies. The [_Tampa Police Department's Cyber Crimes Unit_](https://www.tampa.gov/police/about-us/investigations-and-support/criminal-investigations), for example, operates under the [_Internet Crimes Against Children (ICAC) Task Force Program_](https://www.icactaskforce.org/). This national network comprises more than 4,500 law enforcement and prosecutorial agencies dedicated to combating online child exploitation. The Tampa Police Department is an active member of the Central Florida Task Force, working alongside other agencies to address these crimes. Our Child pornography 
## Legal Framework and Charges
Under [_Section 847.002 of the Florida Statutes_](http://www.leg.state.fl.us/statutes/index.cfm?App_mode=Display_Statute&URL=0800-0899/0847/0847.html), possession of child pornography is a serious felony offense. Each image or video depicting child sexual exploitation constitutes a separate charge. Convictions can result in lengthy prison sentences, hefty fines, and mandatory registration as a sex offender, which carry lifelong implications.
Under federal law, penalties are equally stringent. Convictions for producing child pornography can lead to mandatory minimum sentences of 15 to 30 years in prison. Offenses involving transportation, receipt, distribution, or possession with intent to distribute child pornography carry mandatory minimum terms of 5 years, with maximum terms up to 20 years. Simple possession can result in up to 10 years in federal prison.
## Prosecution and Sentencing
After evidence is collected, cases are referred to the State Attorney’s Office or federal prosecutors for review and prosecution. Prosecutors assess the evidence, determine appropriate charges, and proceed with legal actions. Sentences vary based on factors such as the number of images possessed, the nature of the material, and the defendant's criminal history. 
## Contact an Experienced Child Pornography Investigations Lawyer in Tampa
The legal investigation process for child pornography offenses in Tampa involves coordinated efforts among various law enforcement agencies, adherence to strict legal protocols, and a commitment to upholding justice. Through diligent investigations and prosecutions, authorities strive to protect children and deter potential offenders.
Child pornography investigations are imperative as creation, distribution or possession of child pornographic material is a grave offense in Tampa, Florida. It cannot be overstated that the legal repercussions for those convicted are severe. The life-altering legal consequences include but are not limited to lengthy prison sentences, significant fines and the requirement to register as a sex offender. If you or someone you know is being investigated for creating, distributing or possessing child pornography, it is vital to seek the professional legal assistance of [_Attorney Will Hanlon_](https://www.criminalattorneytampa.net/william-hanlon.html) and ensure the best possible defense. The legal process is complex, but with knowledgeable representation, defendants can have a fair outcome.
If you are in the Tampa area, call [_Hanlon Law_](https://www.criminalattorneytampa.net/) today at 813-228-7095 or [_contact us_](https://www.criminalattorneytampa.net/contact-us.html) online to set up a consultation.

Tampa Lawyer for Manufacturing Child Pornography
# Tampa Lawyer for Manufacturing Child Pornography
## Manufacture of Child Pornography in Tampa, FL
Child pornography is one of the most severe and unforgivable crimes under state and federal law. In Florida, as well as across the United States, the production, distribution, and possession of child pornography are met with harsh legal penalties due to the profound harm such offenses inflict on children and society. Manufacturing child pornography, in particular, is a criminal act with devastating legal consequences. If you are facing charges related to the creation of child pornography in Tampa, Florida, it is critical to understand the serious repercussions that come with this crime and reach out to a Tampa lawyer for manufacturing child pornography.
## The Legal Definition of Child Pornography
Under both federal and Florida state law, child pornography refers to any visual depiction, including photographs, videos, or digital images, of a minor (someone under 18 years of age) engaged in sexually explicit conduct. Manufacturing child pornography refers to the act of creating or producing such material, whether through photography, videography, or other forms of media. This includes situations where the offender films, photographs, or otherwise creates explicit material involving a child.
## Florida State Law on Child Pornography
In Florida, the creation of child pornography is explicitly prohibited under state statutes. Specifically, [_Section 827.071 of the Florida Statutes_](https://www.flsenate.gov/Laws/Statutes/2018/827.071) criminalizes the production and distribution of child pornography. Violating this law can result in severe criminal penalties, as it is considered a form of child exploitation.
Florida law divides the offense of manufacturing child pornography into varying degrees of severity. The most serious charge is the first-degree felony of producing or possessing child pornography, which can result in the following penalties:
  * **First-Degree Felony** : Producing or possessing child pornography in Florida is a first-degree felony, punishable by up to 30 years in prison, a $10,000 fine, and mandatory registration as a sexual offender. A conviction for a first-degree felony may also result in lifelong supervision, including probation and restrictions on where the convicted individual can live, work, and interact with minors.
  * **Second-Degree Felony** : If the individual produces child pornography by using a minor in their care, custody, or control, they may face a second-degree felony charge, which could lead to up to 15 years in prison and a $10,000 fine.


Moreover, Florida law mandates that convicted offenders must undergo psychological evaluations and complete sex offender treatment programs while on probation or after their release from prison. These measures aim to prevent repeat offenses and ensure that offenders do not pose a future threat to minors.
## Federal Law on Child Pornography
Since manufacturing child pornography is a state and federal crime, the [_Federal Child Protection and Obscenity Enforcement Act_](https://www.congress.gov/bill/100th-congress/house-bill/3889) makes it illegal to produce, distribute, or possess child pornography, with even more severe penalties for individuals convicted under federal law. If convicted of manufacturing child pornography, the penalties include:
  * **Mandatory Minimum Sentences** : Under federal law, producing child pornography is a mandatory minimum 15-year sentence with the possibility of up to 30 years in prison. The specific sentence will depend on the number of offenses, the age of the child, and the circumstances surrounding the production of the pornography.
  * **Life Imprisonment** : In cases involving aggravated circumstances—such as the abuse of particularly young children or producing pornography involving multiple minors—the offender may face a life sentence in prison.
  * **Sex Offender Registration** : Like Florida law, federal law mandates that individuals convicted of manufacturing child pornography be placed on the [_national sex offender registry_](https://www.nsopw.gov/). This requirement can severely limit the offender’s ability to live a normal life after serving their sentence, as they will be subject to significant restrictions on where they can live, work, and travel.
  * **Fines and Civil Penalties** : Federal law also imposes substantial fines for individuals convicted of manufacturing child pornography, in addition to any restitution to victims or their families.


## Additional Legal Consequences
Aside from the criminal penalties outlined above, individuals convicted of manufacturing child pornography in Tampa, FL, face additional long-term consequences:
  1. **Social and Emotional Consequences** : Convicted individuals are likely to face social stigma, isolation, and rejection from family, friends, and their community. The long-term emotional toll of a conviction for such a crime can be devastating, not only for the individual but also for their loved ones.
  2. **Civil Lawsuits** : Victims of child pornography often pursue civil lawsuits against those who produce or distribute such material. In some cases, the convicted individual may be ordered to pay substantial damages to the victim and their family.
  3. **International Implications** : Because child pornography is illegal worldwide, individuals convicted under U.S. law may face restrictions on traveling abroad or even be detained in other countries that have strict anti-child exploitation laws.


## The Importance of Legal Defense
Given the seriousness of the penalties for manufacturing child pornography, it is imperative for anyone facing these charges in Tampa, FL, to seek immediate legal counsel. An experienced criminal defense attorney can help navigate the complexities of Florida and federal law, investigate the evidence, and ensure that the defendant’s rights are protected throughout the legal process. While the legal landscape is unforgiving, a skilled attorney may be able to negotiate reduced charges or sentences, depending on the circumstances of the case.
## Contact an Experienced Child Pornography Lawyer in Tampa
Manufacturing child pornography is a grave offense in Tampa, Florida, and the legal repercussions for those convicted are severe. From lengthy prison sentences to significant fines and the requirement to register as a sex offender, the consequences are life-altering. If you or someone you know is facing charges of manufacturing child pornography, it is essential to seek the professional legal assistance of [_Attorney Will Hanlon_](https://www.criminalattorneytampa.net/william-hanlon.html) to understand the full scope of the charges and ensure the best possible defense. The legal process can be complex, but with the right representation, defendants can work toward achieving a fair resolution.

Tampa Lawyer for Possession of Child Pornography Charges
# Tampa Lawyer for Possession of Child Pornography Charges
Being charged with possession of child pornography in Tampa, Florida, is an extremely serious legal matter with life-altering consequences. Under both Florida law and federal statutes, anyone convicted of these charges faces serious penalties such as prison sentences and mandatory sex offender registration.
If you are facing charges, it is crucial to seek immediate legal representation from an experienced Tampa lawyer for possession of child pornography charges. Hanlon Law understands the gravity of these allegations and provides an aggressive, strategic defense to protect your rights, reputation, and future. 
## Possession of Child Pornography Charges in Tampa, Florida
Under [_Florida Statute 827.071_](https://www.flsenate.gov/Laws/Statutes/2018/827.071), possession of child pornography is classified as a felony offense. The law prohibits knowingly possessing, viewing, or distributing any image, video, or other material that depicts a minor (anyone under 18) engaged in sexually explicit conduct.
A conviction for possessing child pornography in Florida can result in:
  * Up to 15 years in prison (for each count)
  * Fines up to $10,000
  * Mandatory sex offender registration
  * Probation with strict restrictions


Additionally, each image or video can be charged as a separate offense, meaning defendants may face multiple felony counts and potentially decades in prison.
If the case involves distribution or production of child pornography, the penalties increase significantly. Federal charges may also apply, resulting in even harsher sentencing guidelines.
## Federal Penalties for Possession of Child Pornography
If a case involves interstate or online activity, the federal government may take over prosecution. Federal law ([_18 U.S.C. § 2252_](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/18/2252) and [_§ 2252A_](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/18/2252A)) imposes strict penalties for possession, distribution, and production of child pornography.
Convictions can lead to:
  * Mandatory minimum 5-year prison sentence (up to 20 years)
  * Large fines and asset forfeiture
  * Supervised release for life


For those with prior convictions or cases involving large amounts of illegal material, penalties can escalate to life in prison.
## Legal Defenses Against Child Pornography Charges
A possession of child pornography charge does not guarantee a conviction. Several potential defense strategies may be available, depending on the circumstances of your case:
  1. **Lack of Knowledge or Intent:** The prosecution must prove that the defendant knowingly possessed illegal material. If the files were downloaded unknowingly, sent without consent, or stored automatically without knowledge, this may serve as a defense.
  2. **Illegal Search and Seizure:** Law enforcement must follow strict protocols when obtaining digital evidence. If authorities conducted an unlawful search, violated your Fourth Amendment rights, or lacked probable cause, evidence could be suppressed in court.
  3. **False Allegations** : False accusations may arise from personal disputes, mistaken identity, or coercion. A skilled defense attorney can investigate the credibility of allegations and uncover weaknesses in the prosecution’s case.
  4. **Third-Party Access or Hacking** : If unauthorized individuals accessed a defendant’s computer, phone, or network, it may be possible to prove that the illegal material was not in their control or possession.
  5. **Insufficient Evidence** : The prosecution must present clear and convincing evidence that the accused knowingly downloaded, stored, or viewed illegal content. Weak or circumstantial evidence may not meet the burden of proof required for a conviction.


## Consequences Beyond Criminal Penalties
A child pornography conviction of any kind has devastating long-term consequences. In addition to criminal penalties, individuals convicted of these offenses must register as sex offenders, which imposes strict restrictions on:
  * Where they can live and work
  * Internet and technology usage
  * Personal and professional relationships
  * Travel and residency options


Being labeled as a sex offender can permanently damage your reputation, career, and personal life. That is why immediate legal representation is critical.
## How Hanlon Law Can Help
If you or a loved one is facing possession of child pornography charges in Tampa, Florida, you need an experienced and aggressive defense attorney on your side. 
[_Hanlon Law_](https://www.criminalattorneytampa.net/) provides:
  * Comprehensive case analysis: We examine every detail of your case to identify weaknesses in the prosecution’s evidence.
  * Aggressive defense strategies: We challenge illegal searches, false allegations, and lack of intent to get charges reduced or dismissed.
  * Experienced trial representation: We fight relentlessly to protect your rights in court.


With years of experience handling complex criminal cases, Hanlon Law is prepared to defend your freedom and future.

Testifying in Your Own Case
# Testifying in Your Own Case
### Criminal Defense Lawyer Representing Tampa Area Residents
If you have been [arrested](https://www.criminalattorneytampa.net/arrests.html) and charged with a crime, you cannot be forced to testify in your own case. The burden is on the prosecutor to prove your guilt beyond a reasonable doubt. You can remain silent, and in many cases, it is wise to do so. When you choose to testify, the prosecutor has an opportunity to cross-examine or impeach you, and can use your testimony to incriminate you and further her case. If you are contemplating going to trial, an experienced Tampa criminal defense attorney can assess your situation and help you make a strategic, well-informed decision about whether to testify.
### The Fifth Amendment and Testifying in Your Own Case
Most Americans know from pop culture that they have a right not to answer police questions while in custody. The Fifth Amendment protects you from having to incriminate yourself in custody, but also in court while on [trial](https://www.criminalattorneytampa.net/right-to-jury-trial.html). You have the right to remain silent at trial and can’t be compelled to serve as a witness against yourself at the behest of the prosecutor or judge or even your own attorney.
If you decide you take the stand, however, you can’t choose to answer some questions and not others. You are required to answer all of them, including questions intended to make you look dishonest or to paint you as a serial criminal. After you’ve decided to testify in your own case, you are considered to have waived the Fifth Amendment right for the whole trial. The [jury](https://www.criminalattorneytampa.net/how-a-jury-functions.html) is not supposed to take your refusal to testify into consideration when determining whether you are guilty.
### Should You Testify?
You should talk to your lawyer about whether it makes sense to testify in your own case. Always be aware that the prosecutor is not your attorney. You cannot trust the prosecutor to do what you think is right. Your own lawyer has a duty of loyalty towards you, and can provide recommendations about whether you are a good candidate to testify on your own behalf.
There are a number of ways in which you have to be particularly careful when testifying on the stand. When the prosecutor asks you a question, it is crucial to listen carefully and only answer if you understand the question. You can request to have confusing questions rephrased rather than answering a question that isn’t clear to you. It’s also important to only answer the question you’re being asked. Often witnesses on the stand give answers that provide details they weren’t asked, and such answers may allow the prosecutor to see an angle he or she missed. When you’re questioned by your own attorney, keep in mind that your attorney may be avoiding asking you a question or asking questions in a certain way in order to avoid certain subjects or answers.
Throughout direct examination and cross-examination, the prosecutor will be alert to possible inconsistencies in testimony. You may be questioned further or made to look bad if there are inconsistencies or places within the testimony where you answered differently at different times.
### Impeachment
The prosecutor may try to impeach you, or raise doubts about whether you are credible. When you testify, your credibility is at issue just as other witnesses’ credibility is at issue when they testify. As a means of impeaching you, a prosecutor can raise prior criminal convictions involving dishonesty or that were punishable by incarceration for more than a year or death. Upon learning about these past convictions, the jury might infer that not only are you dishonest, but also that you committed the crime at issue.
### Consult a Skillful Criminal Defense Lawyer in Tampa
Testifying in your own case in Tampa is a serious strategic decision that can have substantial consequences. If you’ve been charged with a crime, it is wise to consult and hire a knowledgeable criminal defense attorney who understands how to present your defense, and who can evaluate whether you should go to trial or seek a [plea](https://www.criminalattorneytampa.net/plea-agreements.html) deal. Our founder and principal Will Hanlon has defended the accused since 1994, and has significant trial experience. Please contact us at 813-228-7095 or via our online form.

Transmission of Child Pornography by Electronic Device in Tampa, FL
# Transmission of Child Pornography by Electronic Device in Tampa, FL
Facing charges of transmission of child pornography by electronic device is one of the most serious and life-altering legal situations a person can encounter. In Tampa, Florida, these cases are prosecuted aggressively under both federal and state law. They often involve digital evidence, undercover investigations, and mandatory prison sentences.
At [Hanlon Law](https://www.criminalattorneytampa.net/), we know how overwhelming these allegations can be and are committed to providing skilled, strategic criminal defense to protect your rights and your future. If you have been charged, it is vital to promptly retain a Tampa lawyer for transmission of child pornography by electronic device.
### Florida and Federal Laws on Electronic Transmission
Under [Florida Statute § 847.0137](https://www.flsenate.gov/Laws/Statutes/2018/847.0137), it is a felony to electronically transmit child pornography using any device capable of internet access, including computers and smartphones. This law defines transmission broadly, including sharing, sending, posting, or uploading material that depicts a minor engaged in sexual conduct. On the federal level, [18 U.S.C. § 2252](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/18/2252) governs similar conduct, especially if it involves interstate communications or file transfers. Federal charges often result in mandatory minimum sentences ranging from 5 to 15 years, depending on aggravating factors such as prior offenses or the age of the child.
Digital forensics plays a central role in building the government’s case. Prosecutors often rely on:
  * IP addresses
  * Device metadata and file timestamps
  * Chat logs and screen captures
  * Cloud storage and app data


While such evidence can appear definitive, it's not always reliable or accurate. Devices can be shared, hacked, or infected with malware that downloads content automatically. At Hanlon Law, we collaborate with forensic experts to analyze the evidence independently and expose weaknesses in the prosecution’s case.
### Tampa, Florida Sting Operations and Entrapment Defenses
Many cases involving electronic transmission originate from sting operations, where law enforcement officers pose as minors or other individuals in online environments. While these tactics are legal in many instances, they can lead to entrapment. Entrapment is an unlawful situation where a person is coerced or induced into committing a crime they otherwise would not have committed. If entrapment is suspected, Hanlon Law will aggressively challenge the legality of the sting and the conduct of the investigators involved.
A conviction for transmitting child pornography by electronic device can result in:
  * Lengthy prison sentences (state and federal)
  * Mandatory sex offender registration for life
  * Loss of employment and professional licenses
  * Social and reputational ruin
  * Restrictions on housing, travel, and internet use


Even an arrest without conviction can cause irreversible damage to your personal and professional life. These charges carry immense stigma, and the legal process can be harsh and unforgiving.
### Importance of Hiring a Skilled Tampa Sex Crimes Lawyer
At Hanlon Law, we understand that being accused does not equal being guilty. We take the time to thoroughly assess your case, listen to your version of events, and develop a defense strategy tailored to your situation. Common defenses may include:
  * Lack of intent or knowledge
  * Improper search and seizure
  * Technical errors or misidentification
  * Entrapment by law enforcement


If you or someone you care about is facing charges for the transmission of child pornography by electronic device in Tampa ,Florida, don’t delay in seeking legal help. The earlier you involve a defense attorney, the more options you may have for reducing or dismissing the charges.
At Hanlon Law, we offer [confidential consultations](https://www.criminalattorneytampa.net/contact-us.html) and personalized legal strategies to defend your rights in the most challenging legal situations. Whether we’re negotiating with prosecutors, filing motions to suppress evidence, or preparing for trial, our priority is to secure the best possible outcome for you. Contact Hanlon Law today by phone at [(813) 228-7095](tel:\(813\)%20228-7095) or schedule a consultation online.

Tampa Traveling to Meet a Minor Lawyer
# Tampa Traveling to Meet a Minor Lawyer
## Traveling to Meet a Minor Attorney in Tampa, FL
### Sex Crime Lawyer Representing Residents of Tampa
The criminal act of “traveling to meet a minor” is a serious offense in Florida. As a second-degree felony, it is punishable by up to 15 years in state prison. However, simply being charged with this or any other crime is not tantamount to a finding of guilty. Depending upon the situation, the State may ultimately be unable to prove guilt beyond a reasonable doubt, or you may be able to assert an effective defense. Tampa [sex crime](https://www.criminalattorneytampa.net/sex-crimes.html) lawyer Will Hanlon has been defending people accused of many different types of sex crimes, drug crimes, and violent crimes for more than 20 years. He is here to help you understand the legal process and explore possible defenses under the facts of your particular case.
Under Florida Statutes § 847.0135(4), it is unlawful to travel within the State of Florida for the purpose of engaging in unlawful sexual conduct with a child. It is also illegal to travel to Florida or from Florida for this purpose. An “attempt” to travel to meet a minor is also illegal, as is causing someone else to travel to meet a minor or even attempt to do so. It is not a requirement of the statute that the person whom the defendant seeks to meet is actually a minor, as long as the defendant believes that the person is a child based on information obtained from the internet or another electronic data transmission, such as a text message.
### Defenses to the Crime of Traveling to Meet a Minor
It is not unusual for law enforcement officers to set up “sting” operations in an attempt to combat the serious problem of pedophiles and child predators. Sometimes, however, the net is cast too widely, and innocent people who had no intent of engaging in criminal conduct get caught up in the operation. Although the mere fact that an undercover operative or law enforcement officer was involved in the detection or investigation of the alleged crime does not constitute a defense to the crime of traveling to meet a minor, the defense of entrapment may apply in some cases. Entrapment may be either subjective or objective, depending upon whether the police allegedly engaged in egregious conduct. In either case, the defendant must be able to offer proof to support the defense.
Constitutional defenses such as a violation of due process or a failure to advise the defendant of their right to remain silent in custody may also assist a defendant with securing a reduction in the charges brought by the State or possibly having the charges dropped. It is critical that the defendant act quickly in determining any defenses that they may be able to assert because a conviction of the crime of traveling to meet a minor is considered a felony in the second degree in Florida. A second-degree felony is punishable by a term of imprisonment of up to 15 years and a fine of up to $10,000.
### Schedule a Consultation with a Sex Crime Attorney in Tampa
A conviction of any felony offense may create lifelong hardships for the defendant, and this is particularly true for sex crimes. A defendant convicted of traveling to meet a minor will probably spend the rest of their life as a registered sex offender, which not only is embarrassing but also has the potential to affect the defendant’s housing and employment opportunities for decades to come. This may happen even if the defendant never laid a hand on a minor and even if there in fact was no minor. Online sex sting operations are becoming increasingly common in the Tampa Bay region and elsewhere. If you have been arrested as part of such an operation or believe that you are under investigation, contact knowledgeable Tampa attorney Will Hanlon at Hanlon Law [online](https://www.criminalattorneytampa.net/contact-us.html) or at 813-228-7095 for a consultation. He can also help people who need a prostitution defensecriminalattorneytampa attorney or assistance in fighting other sex crime charges.

Tampa Unnatural or Lascivious Act Lawyer
# Tampa Unnatural or Lascivious Act Lawyer
## Unnatural or Lascivious Act Attorney in Tampa, FL
### Sex Crime Lawyer Assisting Defendants in Tampa
In Florida, it is a strict liability crime to intentionally touch a child who is under the age of 16 in a lewd or lascivious way. It is also a crime to solicit someone under the age of 16 to perpetrate a lewd or lascivious act. Lewd or lascivious acts are those that involve lust, sensual intent, or wicked, unchaste, or licentious behavior on the part of the person who is acting. It is not a defense to felony charges to say that you did not know that the child was of a particular age or that the child consented. However, in some cases, it is possible to negotiate away from a serious lewd and lascivious charge by pleading guilty to the crime of an unnatural or lascivious act. Tampa [sex crime](https://www.criminalattorneytampa.net/sex-crimes.html) lawyer Will Hanlon can advise you on whether this may be an option in your case.
### Unnatural or Lascivious Acts
Under Florida Statute section 800.02, a prosecutor can obtain a conviction for an unnatural or lascivious act by proving that the defendant committed an unnatural and lascivious act with another person. Each of these elements needs to be proven beyond a reasonable doubt. This law is vague, and in the past, it was used to prosecute people for acts that would now be considered private matters, such as homosexual acts. What is unnatural? What is lascivious? "Unnatural" describes an act that is not in accord with normal feelings. "Lascivious" describes acts that are done with an intent that is wicked, lustful, not chaste, sensual, or licentious. Often, this law is used to charge someone for engaging in sexual activity in a public place, such as a park.
A mother breastfeeding a baby is not considered a violation that would qualify for criminal charges under section 800.02. An unnatural and lascivious act is charged as a misdemeanor of the second degree. This type of crime can be punished with a maximum of 60 days of jail time and a fine of up to $500.
Generally, the crime is considered a lesser-included offense for other crimes, and as a result, we can possibly use it to negotiate a plea deal if you are charged with some other, bigger crime. Some crimes are necessarily included offenses, and others are permissibly included offenses. The former are those crimes that are always committed if the person commits the larger crime. The latter are sometimes committed if somebody commits the larger crime.
An unnatural and lascivious act is a lesser included offense of the following types of larger crimes: lewd or lascivious battery, lewd or lascivious molestation, lewd or indecent assault on or in the presence of a child, exposure of sexual organs, lewd or lascivious conduct, lewd or lascivious exhibition in a child's presence, or lewd or lascivious offenses committed in the presence of a disabled or elderly person. While an unnatural and lascivious act is charged as a second-degree misdemeanor, these other larger crimes are felonies. Sometimes the evidence for a felony charge involving lewd or lascivious behavior is not quite as strong as it should be, or a prosecutor does not think that the case is worth the expense and effort of pursuing a conviction at trial. In that case, it may be possible to negotiate to plead guilty to an unnatural and lascivious act charge in order to get the other charge that carries the greater sentence dismissed.
### Consult a Skillful Tampa Attorney During a Sex Crime Prosecution
If you are accused of a sexual act involving a minor or an unnatural or lascivious act in the Tampa area, you should retain an attorney right away. In some cases, it makes sense to plead to an unnatural or lascivious act, while in other cases, it makes sense to fight this particular charge because it is so vague. Unlike some other crimes, sex crimes may require you to register as a sex offender, and it is imperative that you have a strong defense and an experienced attorney fighting for you. Our founder, Will Hanlon, has provided a strong, aggressive defense to people accused of sex crimes since 1994. He strives to provide responsive and personalized representation. Call Hanlon Law at 813-228-7095 or use our [online form](https://www.criminalattorneytampa.net/contact-us.html) to set up an appointment if you are facing charges related to lewd or [lascivious molestation](https://www.criminalattorneytampa.net/lewd-and-lascivious-molestation.html) or a similar offense.

Transmission of Child Pornography To A Minor Charges in Tampa, FL
# Transmission of Child Pornography To A Minor Charges in Tampa, FL
Being charged with transmission of child pornography to a minor in Tampa, Florida, is a serious legal situation with potentially life-changing consequences. This offense typically involves the alleged act of sending, sharing, or otherwise transmitting sexually explicit images, videos, or other media involving minors to another person who is also a minor. 
These charges often come with emotionally charged investigations, aggressive prosecution, and stiff penalties, both under Florida law and federal statutes. If you or someone you know is being investigated or has been charged, it is essential to retain an experienced Tampa lawyer for transmission of child pornography to a minor. At [Hanlon Law](https://www.criminalattorneytampa.net/), we provide a strong, informed, and personalized defense for those accused of sex crimes, especially those involving electronic communication and minors.
### What Is Transmission of Child Pornography to a Minor?
Under [Florida Statute § 847.0138](https://www.flsenate.gov/Laws/Statutes/2019/847.0138), any person who knowingly transmits or distributes material that contains sexual conduct by a child to another person, especially a minor, can face felony charges. This includes digital communications over email, text messages, apps, chat rooms, or social media platforms.
On the federal level, [18 U.S. Code § 2252](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/18/2252) and [§ 2252A](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/18/2252A) govern the transportation, distribution, and transmission of child pornography across state lines or through means of interstate or international commerce, including the internet. When the alleged recipient is a minor, or presumed to be one by the defendant, the penalties can be substantially enhanced.
A federal conviction for transmitting child pornography to a minor may carry mandatory minimum sentences starting at 15 years, with potential for much longer terms if aggravating factors exist. These charges can also trigger lifetime registration as a sex offender, loss of civil rights, and devastating damage to your personal and professional life. 
### Tampa, Florida Sex Crimes Attorney
One of the most difficult aspects of defending against charges of transmitting child pornography to a minor is the sheer volume of digital evidence that prosecutors can use to support their case. However, just because material was sent or found on a device does not automatically prove intent or knowledge. 
There are numerous potential defenses that may apply. For instance, a device may be shared among multiple users, or may have been compromised or hacked. Content can be mislabeled, received inadvertently, or downloaded without the user’s full awareness, especially through peer-to-peer networks or malicious pop-ups. The prosecution must prove criminal intent, not just presence of illegal material. 
Furthermore, if law enforcement acted improperly during an investigation in any way, your rights may have been violated. At Hanlon Law, we take a meticulous approach to examining how the investigation was conducted and whether your constitutional protections were respected.
A major defense strategy in these cases may involve the legal doctrine of entrapment, especially if the investigation was initiated by law enforcement agents posing as minors. Entrapment occurs when an individual is persuaded or coerced by government agents to commit a crime they were not otherwise predisposed to commit. 
It’s not uncommon for law enforcement to initiate contact, escalate conversations, and persistently prompt illegal behavior during online sting operations. If you were manipulated or misled into sending explicit material under such circumstances, your attorney can present a strong entrapment defense. 
Additional defenses may include lack of intent, lack of knowledge, false allegations, or insufficient evidence to prove that you knew the recipient was a minor or that you had knowingly transmitted illegal content.
### Criminal Defense Lawyer in Tampa, Florida
Even being accused of transmitting child pornography to a minor can ruin a person’s life. The stigma, media exposure, and social fallout can lead to job loss and destroyed relationships, among other things.
A conviction compounds those effects with years or decades of incarceration, sex offender registration, restrictions on where you can live or work, and permanent damage to your reputation. Unlike many other criminal charges, sex crime convictions often remain a public record and follow individuals for life. 
If you’re under investigation or facing charges related to the transmission of child pornography to a minor, your first call should be to a defense attorney with experience handling serious sex offenses. Not every lawyer has the background or skill required to challenge digital forensics, navigate federal criminal procedure, or identify constitutional issues in evidence gathering. 
At Hanlon Law, we combine legal experience with cutting-edge resources to analyze every aspect of your case from the initial investigation to trial preparation. Whether negotiating a favorable plea or mounting an aggressive defense at trial, our goal is to secure the best possible outcome for you.
If you are being investigated or have been charged in Tampa, Florida, contact [Hanlon Law ](https://www.criminalattorneytampa.net/)today by phone at [(813) 228-7095](tel:\(813\)%20228-7095) or [online](https://www.criminalattorneytampa.net/contact-us.html). We offer confidential consultations and will guide you through the process with respect, discretion, and unwavering defense. Your freedom and future deserve nothing less.

Transmission of Child Pornography in Tampa, FL
# Transmission of Child Pornography in Tampa, FL
Being charged with the transmission of child pornography in Tampa, Florida, can be a serious and life-altering event. When prosecuted, these charges carry severe penalties at both the state and federal level, including long prison sentences, substantial fines, and mandatory sex offender registration. If you or someone you know is facing allegations related to the transmission of child pornography, you need an experienced and aggressive Tampa lawyer for transmission of child pornography charges on your side.
At Hanlon Law, we understand how devastating these accusations can be personally, professionally, and legally. Our firm provides strategic, confidential, and results-driven defense for individuals charged with internet sex crimes in Tampa and throughout Florida.
## What Is Transmission of Child Pornography?
Transmission of child pornography involves sending, sharing, distributing, or making available any material that depicts a minor (anyone under the age of 18) engaged in sexually explicit conduct. This includes images, videos, and other digital content shared via:
  * Email or text message
  * Social media platforms
  * Cloud storage services
  * Peer-to-peer file-sharing networks
  * Encrypted messaging apps
  * Any online or digital communication platform


In many cases, individuals charged with this offense are accused of either knowingly sending such material or knowingly making it available for others to download or access.
## Florida Law on Child Pornography Transmission
Under Florida [_Statute 847.0137_](https://www.flsenate.gov/Laws/Statutes/2018/847.0137), the transmission of child pornography is a second-degree felony. A conviction can result in:
  * Up to 15 years in prison per count
  * Up to 15 years of sex offender probation
  * Fines up to $10,000
  * Mandatory sex offender registration
  * Permanent criminal record


Each individual transmission — even if it involves the same image or video — can be charged as a separate offense, which may lead to multiple counts and a significantly higher total sentence.
## Federal Charges for Transmission of Child Pornography
In cases where the transmission involves the internet, email, or interstate communications, the federal government may prosecute under [_18 U.S.C. § 2252_](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/18/2252) or [_§ 2252A_](https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/18/2252A). Federal charges carry even harsher penalties, including:
  * Mandatory minimum sentences of 5 years, up to 20 years for first-time offenders
  * Enhanced penalties for repeat offenders or if the material involves very young children
  * Lifetime supervised release
  * Asset forfeiture and large fines


Federal prosecutors and agents often use sophisticated digital forensics to trace transmissions, even if the content was deleted or shared anonymously.
## Common Defenses Against Transmission Charges
At Hanlon Law, we believe that every defendant has the right to a strong and fair defense, no matter the nature of the charges. Potential legal defenses to transmission of child pornography charges may include:
**1. Lack of Knowledge or Intent**
The prosecution must prove that you knowingly transmitted illegal material. In some cases, individuals may unknowingly download or share content without realizing its nature or illegality.
**2. Unlawful Search or Seizure**
If law enforcement obtained evidence through a search that violated your Fourth Amendment rights, your attorney can seek to have that evidence suppressed in court.
**3. Computer Hacking or Unauthorized Use**
Your devices may have been accessed or controlled remotely without your knowledge, leading to illegal activity being traced back to you unjustly.
**4. Mistaken Identity**
In cases involving IP addresses or online activity, it's possible that someone else used your network or device, especially in shared living situations or unsecured internet connections.
## Why Choose Hanlon Law Today
When facing federal or state charges for the transmission of child pornography in Tampa, your choice of legal representation matters. At Hanlon Law, we offer:
  * Decades of experience handling complex sex crime cases
  * Aggressive and strategic defense tailored to your specific situation
  * Discreet and confidential legal guidance
  * A commitment to protecting your rights, reputation, and future


We know that the prosecution will come prepared, and so will we. Give us a call today at [_(813) 228-7095_](tel:\(813\)%20228-7095), or [_schedule a confidential consultation_](https://www.criminalattorneytampa.net/contact-us.html) online.
